title: Clock, Delta Time, Fixed Steps, Interpolation, and Stalls · Runtime Lens: Variable Step
summary: Movement is tuned per frame, a long pause launches the ship across the map, collision results change with monitor refresh rate, and catch-up updates freeze the process. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the variable step increment in bounded deterministic time-step policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.
Call Clock.tick once per presented frame, convert milliseconds to seconds, and distinguish measured, capped, and simulation time.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Variable Step, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded deterministic time-step policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-variable-step — Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-time-step-simulation-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-time-step-simulation-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_time_step_simulation_2(frame_seconds: float = 0.016, max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1, fixed_step: float = 1 / 120, accumulator: float = 0.0, max_updates: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if frame_seconds < 0.0 or fixed_step <= 0.0:
        return False, frame_seconds, "time-domain"
    clamped = min(frame_seconds, max_frame_seconds)
    updates = int((accumulator + clamped) / fixed_step)
    if updates > max_updates:
        return False, updates, "spiral-guard"
    if clamped > max_frame_seconds:
        return False, clamped, "clamp-failure"
    assert 0 <= updates <= max_updates
    return True, updates, "bounded-time-step"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-variable-step-oa2t89
# Competency: asteroids-variable-step.
# Case 4016: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_variable_step_oa2t89(frame_seconds: float = 0.016, max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1, fixed_step: float = 1 / 120, accumulator: float = 0.0, max_updates: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_oa2t89 = "oa2t89-98t2ao-4016"
    assert evidence_variant_oa2t89
    evidence_axes_oa2t89 = (
        "playeroa2t89",
        "seed4016",
        "input98t2ao",
        "frameoa2t894016",
        "state4016oa2t89",
        "playtest98t2ao4016",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_oa2t89)) == 6
    if frame_seconds < 0.0 or fixed_step <= 0.0:
        return False, frame_seconds, "time-domain"
    clamped = min(frame_seconds, max_frame_seconds)
    updates = int((accumulator + clamped) / fixed_step)
    if updates > max_updates:
        return False, updates, "spiral-guard"
    if clamped > max_frame_seconds:
        return False, clamped, "clamp-failure"
    assert 0 <= updates <= max_updates
    return True, updates, "bounded-time-step"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Variable Step model · Theory Variable Step
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Variable Step: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 8660 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Variable Step model · Theory Variable Step.
steps.why: Retrieving Variable Step through the concrete bounded deterministic time-step policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Variable Step model · Theory Variable Step, keep Variable Step connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Variable Step model · Theory Variable Step, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Variable Step precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Variable Step model · Theory Variable Step, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 8660 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound delta time, fixed simulation updates, interpolation debt, and the spiral-of-death guard; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Use variable delta time only where stability and determinism evidence justify it and test multiple presentation rates.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Variable Step model · Theory Variable Step, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Multiplying by delta time makes every simulation frame-rate independent.
